rulings.Case Title: ELAINE GINSBERG VS. YENNY MENDEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 22, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT EDUARDO PANIAGUA, and, PANIAGUA CONSTRUCTION INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts that show complaint is not barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP section 335.1). (Issue of res judicata not addressed without prejudice to being raised. Insufficient showing in this demurrer. Defendantt must specify how and why res judicata is applicable, request judicial notice of specific pleadings, and attach copies of all pleadings to be compared and considered by the Court.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ABBIE MARIA SANDEN VS. CALIFORNIA PHYSICIAN'S SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 22, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication is denied. The motion as to the breach of contract cause of action is denied as a matter of law. The Home Care Services Provision and Exclusion #21 are ambiguous and construed against defendant. The motion is denied as to the bad faith and the claim for punitive damages because there is a triable issue of fact regarding whether defendant acted reasonably and with malice. (Dr. Battin deposition 55:7-15; Defendant's responses to plaintiff's UMF #28-39. Dr. Battin assumed the problem were behavioral without much investigation.)=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ABBIE MARIA SANDEN VS. CALIFORNIA PHYSICIAN'S SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 22, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF ABBIE SANDEN, THERESA SANDEN, ABBIE SANDEN, ANNIE SANDEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication is denied as procedurally infirm. Pursuant to CCP section 437c(f)(1), a court can summarily adjudicate whether a duty is or is not owed. See Linden Partners vs. Wilshire Linden Associates (1998) 64 Cal.App. 4th 508, 522. Plaintiff's notice of motion seeks a ruling on Breach. The Court cannot grant the motion for summary adjudication as framed. =(302/CWW)
